chapter_title.ar: باب تَفْضِيلِ الْقَرَابَةِ فِي الزَّكَاةِ وَ مَنْ لَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُعْطَوْا مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 33 – Preferring of the near relatives regarding the Zakāt, and the ones who are not allowed from them that they should be given from the Zakāt

text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) رَجُلٌ مِنْ مَوَالِيكَ لَهُ قَرَابَةٌ كُلُّهُمْ يَقُولُ بِكَ وَ لَهُ زَكَاةٌ أَ يَجُوزُ لَهُ أَنْ يُعْطِيَهُمْ جَمِيعَ زَكَاتِهِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, and Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Abdullah Bin Ja’far, from Ahmad Bin Hamza who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassanasws, ‘A man from the ones in yourasws Wilayah has relatives for him, all of them are saying (believing) in youasws, and for him is Zakāt (to be given out). Is it allowed for him that he gives them the entirety of his Zakāt?’ Heasws said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya and Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah have narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn Ja‘far from Ahmad ibn Hamzah who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is one of your followers whose relatives all speak of you and he owes Zakat; can he pay all of his Zakat to them. He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so.’”
